summary: Created the execution plan synthesizing all 6 dependency artifacts (restore-map, system-audit, knowledge-audit, intake-contract, routing-and-titling-rules, skill-packaging-and-export-policy) into a 7-phase execution sequence with explicit approval gates, rollback points, verification commands, and trap-door questions for V. Resolved all 3 decision points: DP-1 (rebuild SQLite from filesystem truth, not patch), DP-2 (activate future-source adapters AFTER intake redesign is validated), DP-3 (create skill scaffolding BEFORE live code changes). Phases are: 0-Preflight, 1-Historical Restore, 2-SQLite Sync, 3-Skill Creation, 4-Gate+Archive Wiring, 5-Future-Source Adapters, 6-Knowledge Elevation, 7-Export Validation.
learnings: The system-audit revealed 4 SQLite databases in varying states of decay — rebuilding from filesystem truth is always safer than patching when manifests are richer than DB tables

Skill creation must be sequenced before pipeline code changes, not after — otherwise packaging becomes an afterthought and the boundary between reusable logic and private data gets blurred during implementation
Future-source backfill (Fireflies webhooks, Fathom, Pocket) should never precede intake contract validation — non-conforming content entering a pipeline in flux creates compounding normalization debt
7 trap-door questions identified that require V's explicit decision before mutation — surfacing these upfront prevents mid-execution blocks
